Class AbstractStopwatch

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.util.stopwatch.AbstractStopwatch
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable, Stopwatch
Direct Known Subclasses:
InstantStopwatch
public abstract class AbstractStopwatch extends Object implements Stopwatch
Base implementation of Stopwatch interface.

    • state

      protected Stopwatch.State state
      State of this stopwatch

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractStopwatch

      protected AbstractStopwatch()
      Constructor for subclasses to call.

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts or resumes the stopwatch.
      Specified by:
      start in interface Stopwatch
      Throws:
      IllegalStopwatchStateException - if the stopwatch is already running.
      "Implementation Note:"
      This is just a base implementation. Always override this method and call superclass method.

    • pause

      public void pause()
      Pauses the stopwatch. This method doesn't clear the stopwatch stats, unlike the Stopwatch.stop() method.
      Specified by:
      pause in interface Stopwatch
      Throws:
      IllegalStopwatchStateException - if the stopwatch is not running.
      See Also:
      • Stopwatch.State.RUNNING
      "Implementation Note:"
      This is just a base implementation. Always override this method and call superclass method.

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the stopwatch and clear its stats.
      Specified by:
      stop in interface Stopwatch
      Throws:
      IllegalStopwatchStateException - if the stopwatch is already stopped.
      "Implementation Note:"
      This is just a base implementation. Always override this method and call superclass method.

    • getState

      public Stopwatch.State getState()
      Returns the state of this Stopwatch.
      Specified by:
      getState in interface Stopwatch
      Returns:
      the state of this Stopwatch
      See Also:
      • Stopwatch.State

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this Stopwatch.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string representation of this Stopwatch.
      "Implementation Note:"
      By default, this method returns: 
      getState() + " " + String.format("%02d:%02d:%02d.%03d", timeElapsed() / 3600000, timeElapsed() / 60000 % 60, timeElapsed() / 1000 % 3600, timeElapsed() % 1000)
